notes: Send 5 starting villagers to sheep and 1 to stone. Military Schools require stone, so start it immediately.
Scout for sheep, gold, and your opponent.
Put early new villagers onto stone until you have 100 stone for the first Military School.
Use starting wood for the House and Military School; add a Lumber Camp once the school is started.
At 100 stone plus 100 wood, build the first Military School and set it to Spearmen or Archers.
After the school starts, move most stone villagers back to food and wood.
Send 2 villagers to gold with a Mining Camp for the Feudal landmark.
With about 400 food and 200 gold, age up to Feudal. Twin Minaret is the safer beginner economy landmark.
Keep the free Military School unit queued while the landmark goes up.
Gather food and gold for units; keep one villager on stone if you want the second Military School quickly.
In Feudal, add a Stable or Archery Range to match the free unit your Military School is producing.
Scout first: Horsemen punish Archers, Archers punish Spearmen, Spearmen stop cavalry.
Put 2 villagers back on stone before you plan the second Military School.
Use your free plus trained units to raid enemy villagers, then leave before defenders trap you.
Build the second Military School when the stone is ready; free units stack up fast over time.
Spend Vizier points on the bonus that supports your unit plan.
Move to Castle Age and add Janissaries backed by a Mehter for combat auras.
Keep Military Schools running in the background while your paid production makes the main army.
